tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low urine specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible renal dysfunction (likely glomerular) and impaired tubular reabsorption. Low HbA1c and normal serum glucose make diabetes unlikely. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate underlying renal or metabolic pathology. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further workup for renal disease.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
